prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on benzene (12 atoms: 6 C, 6 H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell RKS). Exact four-centre integrals (density fitting not enabled). Geometry taken from the user-supplied .xyz file as-is (single point; not re-optimised at this level).

SCF converged in 8 cycles. Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. No warnings reported.

Headline result — HOMO–LUMO gap: 6.6310857 eV.

Key orbital energies:
  HOMO-2 (index 18): −9.4875 eV
  HOMO-1 (index 19): −7.0473 eV
  HOMO   (index 20): −7.04683 eV
  LUMO   (index 21): −0.41575 eV
  LUMO+1 (index 22): −0.41513 eV
  LUMO+2 (index 23): +1.20219 eV

Total energy: −232.33724 Hartree (−6322.2183 eV). The HOMO/HOMO-1 and LUMO/LUMO+1 pairs are each nearly degenerate (~0.0005–0.0006 eV splitting), consistent with benzene's D6h symmetry (e1g HOMO pair and e2u LUMO pair).

Exact CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1
Output JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpgeywlq8y/live.json
